news.title: UP-RERA makes quarterly transaction reporting mandatory for real estate agents
news.para: Registered real estate agents in Uttar Pradesh will have to maintain books of accounts and customer transaction records in prescribed formats. Delay in filing quarterly transaction reports will attract a fee of ₹10,000 for the corresponding quarter.

news.title: AI, sustainability to shape real estate growth: Experts
news.para: India's real estate sector is poised for significant expansion, driven by artificial intelligence, sustainable building, and regulatory adherence, according to developers at the NAREDCO Real Estate Conclave 2026. Experts emphasized the crucial role of RERA in fostering trust and highlighted emerging opportunities in commercial spaces and data centers.  Addressing a skilled workforce shortage and promoting faster construction methods were also key discussion points for future growth.

news.title: Mahindra Lifespaces acquires 15-acre land in Mumbai; YKK India to set up plant at Chennai industrial park
news.para: Mahindra Lifespace Developers has acquired a 15-acre land parcel in Kandivali East, Mumbai, with an estimated gross development value of ₹5,600 crore. Separately, YKK India will set up a manufacturing facility at Origins by Mahindra, Chennai, with an investment of $150 million.

news.title: Office REIT penetration may rise to 30% by 2030: Report
news.para: Listed REITs and InvITs with real estate assets have an operational portfolio of over 195 million sq ft as of March 2026, according to Colliers India. Office assets under REITs currently stand at about 164 million sq ft, translating into a penetration level of around 19%.

news.title: Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar metro line on track for August commissioning: PITCMRL
news.para: Pune's much-awaited Metro Line 3 is set to open in August 2026. The first phase, covering 12 stations from Maan to RK Laxman Museum, will be ready by June-end. This new line will significantly reduce travel time between Shivajinagar and Hinjewadi.  It promises seamless connectivity and a sustainable transport alternative for thousands of commuters.

news.title: Chennai metro phase-II line yet to open despite getting approval
news.para: Chennai Metro Rail's first Phase-2 line, a 14.6km stretch from Poonamallee Bypass to Vadapalani, has received all necessary approvals but remains uncommissioned four months later. Delays, attributed to elections and government formation, have pushed back the planned launch, leaving commuters in western suburbs waiting for improved connectivity.

news.title: Premium FAR scheme not Akrama-Sakrama by another name: Karnataka HC
news.para: Bengaluru's High Court has upheld the state's Premium Floor Area Ratio scheme. The court ruled it is different from the Akrama-Sakrama scheme. Premium FAR allows increased construction by altering development rules. It does not regularise illegal structures. The scheme benefits new and existing buildings. Transferable Development Rights will remain in demand.

news.title: No new Bengaluru metro constructions in 4.5 years as approvals lag
news.para: Bengaluru's Metro expansion faces a significant halt, with no new corridors entering construction for over four years. Key Phase III and IIIA projects are stalled in the approval pipeline, exacerbated by design changes like double-decker corridors, leading to substantial cost escalations and delayed public transport development.

news.title: Raidurg land row: SBI claims ownership as Telangana government defends auction
news.para: The Telangana High Court has stayed auction proceedings for a 6.2-acre land parcel in Raidurg for three weeks. State Bank of India claims ownership of 5 acres, allotted in 2010, and disputes the state's assertion of offering alternate land and the auction's legality. The state argues SBI failed to develop the land for 16 years.

news.title: Hotel, PGs, offices sealed in Gurugram's DLF-3 for flouting building code
news.para: Enforcement teams in Gurugram launched a large-scale drive against unauthorized constructions and commercial activities in DLF-3, sealing hotels, PGs, offices, and demolishing illegal structures. This action, following court directives, marks the beginning of a wider campaign across DLF phases 1-5 to restore residential areas to their approved land use and building norms.

news.title: Buying land, flats gets costlier as Bihar government raises circle rates
news.para: Bihar has significantly raised land and property registration rates. Rural land values have jumped by 60%, while urban property rates have doubled. These revised minimum value register rates are effective immediately. The government also introduced a 5% annual increase. Women will receive enhanced concessions on property registration. This move aims to boost state revenue and farmer compensation.

news.title: Tamil Nadu government halts ₹2,000 crore Brigade's housing project near Pallikaranai Ramsar site
news.para: Construction on Brigade Enterprises' Rs 2,000-crore housing project in Perumbakkam has been halted. The Tamil Nadu government found work began without mandatory permission from the Tamil Nadu State Wetland Authority. The project is alleged to be within the Pallikaranai Ramsar Wetland Site. The environmental clearance was revoked. Brigade Enterprises plans to appeal the order.

news.title: Over 1.1 lakh applicants vie for 973 plots off Noida airport
news.para: Yamuna Authority received over 1.1 lakh applications for 973 residential plots near Noida International Airport, highlighting a surge in demand. The draw for these plots, reserved for various categories including farmers and industrialists, was conducted amidst significant public interest. This overwhelming response underscores the region's rapid urban expansion and growing economic potential.

news.title: Fast-track RERA cases, ensure transparency: Bihar housing minister
news.para: Bihar's Urban Development Minister Nitish Mishra has directed officials to expedite the resolution of homebuyers' grievances and enhance transparency in the real estate sector. The minister emphasized protecting buyer interests, ensuring timely project completion, and promoting earthquake-resistant construction to foster sustainable urban development and attract investment.

news.title: Mhajo Flat scheme to be rolled out in 15 days: Goa CM
news.para: Goa's 'Mhajo Flat' scheme, launching within 15 days, will resolve a decades-old legal issue for 30,000-40,000 flat owners. Many have lived in their homes for generations but lack formal ownership due to unresolved land titles. The scheme enables the registrar to sign deemed conveyances, granting flat owners their rightful property titles.

news.title: Homebuyers to get builders’ complaint history on Gujarat RERA portal
news.para: GujRERA will soon display builders' past project records and pending complaints on its website to enhance transparency for homebuyers. This move aims to hold developers accountable by linking promoter profiles to new project registrations, potentially denying approvals for those with serious unresolved issues.

news.title: Mumbai court denies pre-arrest bail to businessman in ₹1,000 crore bank fraud
news.para: A Mumbai court denied anticipatory bail to businessman Sudhir Valia, an accused in a Rs 1,000-crore financial fraud linked to Yes Bank. The court cited the seriousness of the alleged economic fraud and the necessity of custodial interrogation for a thorough investigation. Valia and Yes Bank officials are accused of illegally transferring mortgaged property.

news.title: HDFC Property Fund, HDFC Capital Advisors pay ₹26 lakh to settle regulatory violations
news.para: HDFC Property Fund and HDFC Capital Advisors have settled a Sebi case for Rs 26 lakh concerning delayed liquidation and distribution of assets for two schemes. The funds, HIREF and HITCF, faced significant delays, with HIREF taking about seven years to wind up. HDFC Capital Advisors, the current investment manager, ensured the final distribution of retained amounts to investors.

news.title: Homebuyers bear the brunt as property registration stuck over deed workers’ strike in NCR
news.para: Homebuyers in Greater Noida face significant delays in property registration due to an ongoing strike by lawyers and deed writers protesting the UP government's e-registration initiative. Despite a clarification limiting e-registration, the deadlock persists, leaving hundreds of buyers unable to legally own their purchased properties and incurring financial burdens.

news.title: India needs to accelerate construction pace to meet urbanisation demand: Housing minister
news.para: Housing and Urban Affairs Minister Manohar Lal Khattar said every building should carry a durability certificate indicating its expected lifespan. Speaking at Bharat Buildcon 2026, he also called for faster adoption of prefabricated construction, 3D printing, engineered bamboo and recycled construction materials.
